basics.name: Aaron M. Thomas
basics.label: PhD Researcher in Quantum Machine Learning
basics.summary: PhD researcher at the University of Birmingham working on quantum machine learning, generative modelling, and drug discovery. Interested in hybrid quantum–classical architectures on NISQ devices.

work.name: National Institute of Informatics (NII), Tokyo
work.summary: Implementing segmented Eulerised neighbourhood tree algorithms on molecular junction trees for molecular generation on hybrid quantum-classical LLMs.

work.name: QunaSys
work.position: Research Intern — Quantum Machine Learning for Drug Discovery
work.summary: Contributing to the QuEnAIS and FULLMAP consortiums, developing quantum-enhanced AI methodologies for next-generation drug and battery discovery through hybrid quantum–classical modelling.

work.name: DeepWok Lab, Imperial College London
work.position: Research Intern — Parameter Efficient Fine-tuning of LLMs
work.summary: Co-authored an ACL 2025 long paper proposing refined salience-aware sparse fine-tuning strategies that improve parameter efficiency and performance in large language models.

courses: Neural Predictor-Guided Quantum Ansatz Search (MSci Thesis)

awards.title: NISQ Quantum Computing School
awards.summary: Attended the NISQ Quantum Computing School at the University of Sherbrooke and achieved Grade A.

publications.name: VAE-QWGAN: Addressing Mode Collapse in Quantum GANs via Autoencoding Priors
publications.summary: A hybrid quantum–classical generative model leveraging autoencoding priors to mitigate mode collapse and improve sample diversity in quantum GANs.

publications.name: QCA-MolGAN: Quantum Circuit Associative Molecular GAN with Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
publications.summary: Enhanced hybrid quantum generative adversarial networks for drug discovery applications in tandem with multi-agent reinforcement learning.

publications.name: On the Generalization of Adversarially Trained Quantum Classifiers
publications.summary: Analysis of adversarially trained quantum classifiers, deriving novel PAC/Rademacher-based generalization bounds.

publications.name: Refined Salience-Aware Sparse Fine-Tuning of Large Language Models
publications.summary: Salience-aware sparse fine-tuning strategies improving parameter efficiency and performance in large language models.

projects.name: VAE-QWGAN
projects.summary: Hybrid quantum–classical generative model integrating VAE priors with quantum Wasserstein GAN to address mode collapse in quantum generative modelling.

projects.name: QCA-MolGAN
projects.summary: Quantum circuit associative molecular GAN with multi-agent reinforcement learning for drug discovery.
